website_content.title: WB ANM GNM Syllabus 2025
website_content.markdown_content:
## WB ANM GNM Syllabus 2025

The West Bengal Joint Entrance Exam Board (WBJEEB) has released the syllabus for the Auxiliary Nurse and Midwifery (ANM) and General Nursing and Midwifery (GNM) programs for 2025. This syllabus is crucial for candidates preparing for admission to these programs in West Bengal.

**Key Details:**

*   **Exam Pattern:** OMR-based, 100 multiple-choice questions
*   **Duration:** 90 minutes (1.5 hours)
*   **Total Marks:** 115
*   **Category 1:** 1 mark/question, -¼ mark for incorrect answers
*   **Category 2:** 2 marks/question, no negative marking
*   **Languages:** English & Bengali (except Basic English & Logical Reasoning - only in English)

**Syllabus Breakdown:**

*   **Life Science:** Cellular Organization, Ecology, Diversity of Life Forms, etc.
*   **Physical Science:** Matter, Thermal Phenomena, Chemical Calculations, etc.
*   **Mathematics:** Natural Numbers, Integers, Fractions, Percentages, etc.
*   **Basic English:** Adjectives, Reading Comprehension, Sentence Correction, etc.
*   **General Knowledge:** States & Capitals, National Symbols, Indian Rivers, etc.
*   **Logical Reasoning:** Puzzles, Syllogisms, Coding-Decoding, etc.

**Exam Preparation Tips:**

1.  Prioritize Life Science & Physical Science.
2.  Practice with previous year papers and mock tests.
3.  Use Class 10 NCERT books for core subjects.
4.  Revise consistently.

**Exam Date:** October 19, 2025

website_content.title: TANCET 2026 Exam Date Announced
website_content.markdown_content:
## TANCET 2026 Exam Date Announced! 🗓️

The Tamil Nadu Common Entrance Test (TANCET) 2026 exam date is **May 9, 2026**, for both MBA and MCA programs.

**Key Details:**

| Information | Details |
|---|---| 
| **Full Form** | Tamil Nadu Common Entrance Test |
| **Conducting Body** | Anna University |
| **Exam Level** | State-level |
| **Programmes Offered** | MBA, MCA |
| **Exam Mode** | Offline (OMR-based) |
| **Exam Duration** | 2 hours (120 minutes) |
| **Total Questions** | 100 |
| **Total Marks** | 100 |
| **Marking Scheme** | +1 for correct, –0.25 for incorrect |
| **Medium of Exam** | English |
| **MCA Session** | 10:00 AM – 12:00 noon |
| **MBA Session** | 02:30 PM – 04:30 PM |

**Eligibility:**

*   **MBA:** Bachelor's degree in any discipline.
*   **MCA:** Bachelor's degree in Computer Application or B.Sc in IT/Computer Science/related field with Mathematics at 10+2 or graduation level.

**Application Fee:**

*   General/Unreserved: ₹1,000
*   Reserved (SC/SCA/ST): ₹500

**How to Apply:**

1.  Visit the official website: [https://tancet.annauniv.edu/tancet/index.html](https://tancet.annauniv.edu/tancet/index.html)
2.  Click on “Application” or “Register”.
3.  Create a login.
4.  Fill the application form.
5.  Upload documents.
6.  Pay the application fee.
7.  Submit and download confirmation.

**Preparation Tips:**

*   Understand the exam pattern and syllabus.
*   Solve previous year papers.
*   Take timed mock tests.
*   Revise formulas and vocabulary.

------- | --------
Organisation | Punjab Subordinate Services Selection Board (PSSSB)
Posts | Technical Assistant (Advt. 12/2025), Audit Officer (Advt. 01/2025), Legal Advisor, Senior Forensic Supervisor, Assistant Director Fisheries (Advt. 10/2025), Multipurpose Fisheries Skilled Worker (Advt. 09/2025), Librarian, Autoclave Operator/Machine Operator (Advt. 13/2025)
Exam Date | 08 March 2026
Admit Card Status | Released
Mode of Exam | Offline OMR based
Category | Admit Card / Hall Ticket

**Important Dates:**

Event | Date
------- | --------
Admit card release date | 04-03-2026
Written exam date | 08 March 2026

**How to Download:**

1. Visit the official website: [https://sssb.punjab.gov.in](https://sssb.punjab.gov.in).
2. Click on the link titled “Download Admit Card for written examination to be held on 08.03.2026 for Technical Assistant, Audit Officer, Legal Advisor and other posts” (or similar).
3. Enter your Registration/Application Number, Date of Birth and captcha code (if required).
4. Download the PDF and take 2–3 printouts.

**Details on Admit Card:**

*   Candidate’s name and photograph
*   Roll number and registration/application number
*   Name of post
*   Exam date and time
*   Exam centre details
*   Important instructions

**Exam Day Instructions:**

*   Reach the exam centre 60-90 minutes before reporting time.
*   Carry admit card and valid photo ID.
*   Do not carry electronic gadgets.

**In case of issues:**

*   Re-check Registration Number/Date of Birth format.
*   Try clearing cache or using another browser.
*   Contact PSSSB helpdesk.

website_content.markdown_content:
## BPSC Assistant Prosecution Officer Recruitment 2026

**Company:** Bihar Public Service Commission (BPSC)

**Post Name:** Assistant Prosecution Officer (Abhiyojan Padadhikari)

**No. of Posts:** 300

**Advt No:** 13/2026

**Salary:** Pay Level-9 (as per 7th Pay Commission)

**Qualification:** Law Graduate (LLB) with eligibility for Advocate registration

**Age Limit:** 21-37 years (UR Male) as on 01.08.2026 (relaxations applicable - BC/EBC (Male/Female) & UR Female: 40 years, SC/ST (Male/Female): 42 years).

**Start Date:** 27.02.2026

**Last Date:** 20.03.2026

**Website:** www.bpsc.bihar.gov.in

**Vacancy Details:**

| Category | Total Posts |
|---|---|
| Women (35% Horizontal) | 99 |
| Unreserved | 129 |
| EWS | 29 |
| SC | 42 |
| ST | 07 |
| EBC | 64 |
| BC | 25 |
| BC Women | 04 |
| **Total** | **300** |

Freedom Fighters Grandchildren: 06 posts (Horizontal)
PwBD (Horizontal): VI-03, DD-03, OH-03, MD-03

**Selection Process:**

*   Preliminary Exam: Objective Papers (GS-100 marks, Law-150 marks)
*   Main Exam: Descriptive Papers (Total 1000 marks)

website_content.markdown_content:
## CSBC Bihar Police Constable (Operator) Recruitment 2026

The Central Selection Board of Constable (CSBC), Bihar, is recruiting for 993 Constable (Operator) positions in the Bihar Police Radio Organization (Advt. No. 02/2026).

**Key Details:**

*   **Organization:** Central Selection Board of Constable (CSBC), Bihar
*   **Post Name:** Constable (Operator) [Sipahi (Prachalak)]
*   **Job Type:** Regular Government Job
*   **Vacancies:** 993 (Provisional)
*   **Pay Scale:** ₹21,700 - ₹69,100 (Level-3)
*   **Qualification:** 10+2 (Intermediate) in Science with Physics, Chemistry, Math (50% min, 45% for SC/ST)
*   **Application Mode:** Online
*   **Selection Process:** Written Exam, Physical Efficiency Test (PET), Document Verification
*   **Job Location:** Bihar

**Important Dates:**

*   **Notification Release:** February 2026
*   **Online Application Start:** March 1, 2026
*   **Last Date to Apply:** March 31, 2026

**Application Fee:** ₹100 + bank charges

**Age Limit (as of 01.08.2026):**

*   Minimum: 18 years
*   Maximum: 25 years (Unreserved), 27 years (Backward/Extremely Backward Males), 28 years (Backward/Extremely Backward Females), 30 years (SC/ST/Transgender)

**Physical Standards:**

*   **Height:** Varies by gender and category (155cm - 165cm)
*   **Chest (Males):** 81-86cm (Unreserved/Backward), 79-86cm (SC/ST/Extremely Backward)
*   **Weight (Females):** Minimum 48 kg

**PET Details:**

*   **Running:** 1.6 km (Males - 6 min), 1 km (Females - 6 min)
*   **High Jump:** 3ft 6in (Males), 2ft 6in (Females)
*   **Shot Put:** 16lb (Males), 12lb (Females)

For detailed information, refer to the official notification.

website_content.title: HPCL Engineer Recruitment 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## HPCL Engineer Syllabus 2026

Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited (HPCL) has released the detailed syllabus and exam pattern for Engineer posts in various disciplines for recruitment 2026.

**Exam Overview:**

*   **Exam Name:** HPCL Engineer Exam 2026
*   **Conducting Body:** Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited (HPCL)
*   **Post Name:** Engineer (Mechanical, Electrical, Civil, Industrial, etc.)
*   **Exam Mode:** Online (Computer-Based Test - CBT)
*   **Exam Type:** Objective (MCQ)
*   **Total Questions:** 170
*   **Total Marks:** 170
*   **Exam Duration:** 150 Minutes (2 Hours 30 Minutes)
*   **Negative Marking:** No Negative Marking
*   **Selection Process:** Computer-Based Test (CBT) + Group Task + Interview
*   **Exam Language:** English

**Syllabus Breakdown:**

*   **Section I (85 Questions, 85 Marks):** General Aptitude (Logical Reasoning, Quantitative Aptitude, English Language)
*   **Section II (85 Questions, 85 Marks):** Technical / Professional Knowledge (Discipline-specific - Mechanical, Electrical, Civil, Instrumentation, Chemical)

**Key Topics:**

*   **General Aptitude:** Data Interpretation, Logical Reasoning, Reading Comprehension, Percentage & Ratio
*   **Technical:** Core subjects from respective engineering branch (focus on numerical & conceptual questions).

**Important Note:** Always refer to the official notification on [hindustanpetroleum.com](https://hindustanpetroleum.com) for the most up-to-date syllabus and information.

**Download Syllabus PDFs:**

*   [HPCL Chemical Engineering Syllabus 2026 PDF](link_placeholder_chemical)
*   [HPCL Civil Engineering Syllabus 2026 PDF](link_placeholder_civil)
*   [HPCL Electrical Engineering Syllabus 2026 PDF](link_placeholder_electrical)
*   [HPCL Mechanical Engineering Syllabus 2026 PDF](link_placeholder_mechanical)
*   [HPCL Industrial Engineering Syllabus 2026 PDF](link_placeholder_industrial)

website_content.title: IIT JAM 2026 Response Sheet Released
website_content.markdown_content:
## IIT JAM 2026 Response Sheet – Important Update

The response sheet for IIT JAM 2026 has been released online! Candidates can download it via the JOAPS portal using their Enrollment ID/Email ID and password.

**Key Details:**

*   **Exam Name:** Joint Admission Test for Masters (IIT JAM) 2026
*   **Conducting Institute:** Indian Institute of Technology Bombay (IIT Bombay)
*   **Exam Date:** Mid-February 2026 (as per official schedule)
*   **Response Sheet Date:** 18–19 February 2026
*   **Availability:** Online via JOAPS
*   **Login Credentials:** Enrollment ID/Email ID and Password

**Purpose of Response Sheet:**

*   Verify marked answers
*   Estimate probable score

**Next Steps:**

*   Provisional answer key release
*   Result announcement
*   Final answer key release
*   Scorecard availability

**How to Download:**

1.  Visit [jam2026.iitb.ac.in](jam2026.iitb.ac.in).
2.  Click on the JOAPS/“Candidate Login”/“JAM 2026 Response Sheet” link or visit [joaps.iitb.ac.in/login](joaps.iitb.ac.in/login).
3.  Enter your login credentials.
4.  Locate and download the response sheet as a PDF.

website_content.title: HPSC HCS Exam 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## HPSC HCS Syllabus 2026

The Haryana Public Service Commission (HPSC) has released the syllabus for the HCS Exam 2026. This guide covers the exam pattern, marking scheme, and subject-wise topics.

**Exam Overview:**

*   **Exam Name:** HPSC HCS Examination 2026
*   **Conducting Body:** Haryana Public Service Commission (HPSC)
*   **Post Name:** Haryana Civil Services (HCS) & Allied Services
*   **Exam Mode:** Offline (Prelims: OMR, Mains: Descriptive)
*   **Exam Type:** Objective (Prelims), Descriptive (Mains)
*   **Total Questions (Prelims):** 200 (100 each paper)
*   **Total Marks (Prelims):** 200
*   **Total Marks (Mains):** 600
*   **Exam Duration (Prelims):** 4 Hours (2+2)
*   **Exam Duration (Mains):** 3 Hours per paper
*   **Selection Process:** Preliminary Exam + Main Written Exam + Interview
*   **Exam Language:** English / Hindi

**Prelims Exam Pattern:**

| Paper | Subject                      | Marks | Duration |
|-------|------------------------------|-------|----------|
| I     | General Studies              | 100   | 2 Hours  |
| II    | Civil Services Aptitude Test | 100   | 2 Hours  |
| Total |                              | 200   | 4 Hours  |

**Mains Exam Pattern:**

| Paper | Subject                     | Marks | Duration |
|-------|-----------------------------|-------|----------|
| I     | English & English Essay     | 100   | 3 Hours  |
| II    | Hindi & Hindi Essay         | 100   | 3 Hours  |
| III   | General Studies I            | 100   | 3 Hours  |
| IV    | General Studies II           | 100   | 3 Hours  |
| V     | General Studies III          | 100   | 3 Hours  |
| VI    | General Studies IV (Ethics) | 100   | 3 Hours  |
| Total |                             | 600   |          |

**Key Points:**

*   Prelims is a qualifying test.
*   Mains is descriptive.
*   Interview follows Mains.
*   No negative marking for Prelims.

**Download the Syllabus:**

[Click Here to Download HPSC HCS Syllabus PDF 2026 (Official)](https://hpsc.gov.in/)

website_content.title: HSSC Forest Guard Exam 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## HSSC Forest Guard Syllabus 2026

The Haryana Staff Selection Commission (HSSC) has released the detailed syllabus and exam pattern for the Forest Guard Exam 2026. Candidates preparing for the HSSC Forest Guard recruitment can download the syllabus details, exam pattern, and marking scheme.

**Exam Overview:**

*   **Exam Name:** HSSC Forest Guard Exam 2026
*   **Conducting Body:** Haryana Staff Selection Commission (HSSC)
*   **Post Name:** Forest Guard / Forester
*   **Vacancies:** 779
*   **Advt. No.:** 04/2026
*   **Exam Mode:** Offline (OMR-based)
*   **Exam Type:** Objective (MCQ)
*   **Total Questions:** 100
*   **Total Marks:** 100
*   **Exam Duration:** 1 Hour 45 Minutes
*   **Negative Marking:** No
*   **Selection Process:** Written Exam + PST/PET + Document Verification

**Exam Pattern:**

| Subjects / Sections | Weightage | No. of Questions | Marks | Duration |
|---|---|---|---|---| 
| General Awareness, Reasoning, Mathematics, Science, History (Related to Haryana), Current Affairs, Geography, Civics, Environment, Culture, Literature | 20% | 20 | 20 | 1 Hour 45 Minutes |
| Computer terminologies, Computer abbreviations, Hardware and Software, Keyboard shortcuts, Networking, Operating System, Internet, MS Office | 10% | 10 | 10 | |
| Subject Related Topic | 70% | 70 | 70 | |
| **Total** | **100%** | **100** | **100** | |

**Key Highlights:**

*   Each question carries 1 mark.
*   No negative marking.
*   Question paper will be bilingual (English & Hindi).
*   Questions are generally of 10th standard level.

**Important Topics:**

*   Environment, Forest wildlife, Biodiversity, Forest conservation, and Climate change.
*   Haryana GK, Economy, and Environmental Studies.

**Preparation Tips:**

*   Understand the complete syllabus.
*   Create a realistic study timetable.
*   Practice previous year question papers and mock tests.
*   Improve speed and accuracy.
*   Stay updated with current affairs.
*   Maintain physical fitness for PST/PET.

The Delhi Subordinate Services Selection Board (DSSSB) has released the result for the Post Graduate Teacher (PGT) Commerce - Male & Female posts (Post Code 810/23) on **11-03-2026**. 

**Key Details:**

*   **Recruiting Body:** Delhi Subordinate Services Selection Board (DSSSB)
*   **Post Name:** Post Graduate Teacher (PGT) Commerce - Male & Female
*   **Result Type:** Marks Declaration & Shortlisting for e-Dossiers
*   **Result Declaration Date:** 11-03-2026
*   **Exam Date:** 28-29 July 2025
*   **Official Website:** [dsssb.delhi.gov.in](dsssb.delhi.gov.in)

**How to Check Your Result:**

1.  Visit the official website: [dsssb.delhi.gov.in](dsssb.delhi.gov.in) or OARS portal at [dsssbonline.nic.in](dsssbonline.nic.in).
2.  Go to the "Latest Result" or "OARS Module" section.

**Result Contains:**

*   Roll Number / Application Number

*   Category (Gen/OBC/SC/ST/EWS)
*   Qualifying Status
*   Cutoff Marks

**Important for Shortlisted Candidates:**

*   Upload e-dossiers within the specified timeline.
*   Prepare original certificates for verification.

website_content.title: TN SSLC Tamil Exam 2026 - Details & Resources
website_content.markdown_content:
# TN SSLC Tamil Exam 2026 - Details & Resources

The Tamil Nadu State Secondary School Leaving Certificate (SSLC) Tamil exam for March 2026, conducted by the Directorate of Government Examinations (DGE) Tamil Nadu, is a crucial part of the public exams. This guide provides a comprehensive overview of the exam pattern, resources, and sample questions.

## Exam Pattern

*   **Total Marks:** 100
*   **Exam Duration:** 2.5 - 3 hours
*   **Paper I Breakdown:**
    *   Part I: 15 MCQs (1 mark each) - 15 marks
    *   Part II: Short Answers - 18 marks
    *   Part III: Medium Answers - 18 marks
    *   Part IV: Creative Writing - 25 marks
    *   Part V: Essays - 24 marks
*   **Syllabus:** Reduced syllabus for 2025-26, emphasizing grammar (Illakkanam), literature (Kavitha, Natrinai), and comprehension.

## Resources

| Resource           | Description                               | Source       |
| ------------------ | ----------------------------------------- | ------------ |
| Question Paper     | 10th Tamil Public 2026 paper             | [Click Here](https://example.com/questionpaper) |
| Unofficial Answer Key | Detailed solutions for all sections       | [Click Here](https://example.com/answerkey) |

## Sample Questions (Part I)

1.  “இருவர் உரையாடுவது போன்ற ஒசை” என்பது ______. (அ) அகவலோசை (ஆ) துளைலோசை (இ) துங்கலோசை (ஈ) செப்பலோசை - Answer: (ஈ) செப்பலோசை
2.  இரவீந்திரநாத தாகூர் ______ மொழியில் எழுதிய கவிதைத் தொகுப்பான தீதான்சலியை ______ மொழியில், மொழிபெயர்த்த பிறகுதான் அவருக்கு நோபல் பரிசு கிடைத்தது. (அ) ஆங்கில, வங்காள (ஆ) வங்காள, ஆங்கில (இ) வங்காள, தெலுங்கு (ஈ) தெலுங்கு, ஆங்கில - Answer: (ஆ) வங்காள, ஆங்கில

... (More sample questions available in the original text)

## Important Dates

*   **Exam Date:** March 2026
*   **Result Expected:** May 2026

**Note:** Always refer to the official DGE Tamil Nadu website ([https://dge.tn.gov.in](https://dge.tn.gov.in)) for the most accurate and up-to-date information.

website_content.title: IDBI Bank Junior Assistant Manager Recruitment 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## IDBI Bank Junior Assistant Manager (JAM) Grade O Recruitment 2026

**Overview:**

IDBI Bank has announced a recruitment drive for 1100 Junior Assistant Manager (JAM) – Grade “O” (Regional) positions.

**Key Details:**

*   **Organization:** IDBI Bank Ltd.
*   **Post Name:** Junior Assistant Manager (JAM) – Grade “O” (Regional)
*   **Total Vacancies:** 1100
*   **Recruitment Year:** 2026-27
*   **Application Mode:** Online
*   **Cut-off Date for Eligibility:** 01 March 2026
*   **Advertisement Release Date:** 06 March 2026
*   **Online Registration Dates:** 08 March 2026 to 19 March 2026
*   **Application Fee Payment:** 08 March 2026 to 19 March 2026
*   **Tentative Online Test Date:** 12 April 2026 (Morning Session)

**Eligibility Criteria:**

*   **Nationality:** Indian citizen, or subject of Nepal/Bhutan, or Tibetan refugee, or person of Indian origin.
*   **Education:** Bachelor’s degree in any discipline from a recognized University with a minimum of 60% marks (55% for SC/ST/PwBD).
*   **Age Limit:** 20-25 years (as of 01 March 2026). Relaxation applicable as per rules.

**Application Fees:**

*   SC/ST/PwBD: ₹250 (Intimation charges only)
*   All other categories: ₹1050 (Application fee + intimation charges)

**Selection Process:**

1.  Online Test (OT)
2.  Document Verification (DV)
3.  Personal Interview (PI)
4.  Pre Recruitment Medical Test (PRMT)

**Salary:**

₹6.14 lakh to ₹6.50 lakh per annum (CTC) for Class A cities.

**Important Dates:**

*   Eligibility Cut-off: 01 March 2026
*   Advertisement Published: 06 March 2026
*   Registration: 08 March - 19 March 2026
*   Fee Payment: 08 March - 19 March 2026
*   Online Test: 12 April 2026

website_content.title: NIOS Class 10 Business Studies Syllabus 2025-26
website_content.markdown_content:
## NIOS Class 10 Business Studies Syllabus 2025-26 Released! 📚

The National Institute of Open Schooling (NIOS) has released the revised syllabus for Class 10 Business Studies for the academic year 2025-26. This is crucial for students preparing for the October 2025 examinations.

**Key Highlights:**

*   **Exam Duration:** 3 hours
*   **Total Marks:** 100
*   **Modules:** 6 main modules + Practical/Project Work

**Module Breakdown:**

*   Introduction to Business (12 marks)
*   Forms of Business Organization (15 marks)
*   Service Sector (25 marks)
*   Buying, Selling and Distribution (18 marks)
*   Consumer Awareness (18 marks)
*   Career Opportunities in Business (12 marks)

**Exam Pattern:**

| Question Type | Number of Questions | Marks per Question | Total Marks |
|---|---|---|---|
| Objective (MCQ) | 20 | 1 | 20 |
| Objective (Fill in the Blanks, True/False, etc.) | 15 | 2 | 30 |
| Very Short Answer (VSA) | 6 | 2 | 12 |
| Short Answer (SA) | 6 | 3 | 18 |
| Long Answer (LA) | 4 | 5 | 20 |

**Resources Available:**

*   Sample Question Papers
*   Detailed Exam Patterns

**How to Download:**

1.  Visit the official NIOS website.
2.  Navigate to the “Student Information” section.
3.  Select “Secondary Courses”.
4.  Find and click on “Business Studies (215)”.
5.  Choose the 2025-26 syllabus.
6.  Download the PDF.

website_content.title: KMAT Answer Key 2026 Released
website_content.markdown_content:
## KMAT Answer Key 2026 – Download Now!

The KMAT Answer Key 2026 has been released in provisional format for the Session 1 exam held on February 22, 2026. Candidates can download the key to match their responses and estimate their scores.

**Important Dates:**

*   **Exam Date:** February 22, 2026
*   **Answer Key Release:** February 23, 2026
*   **Objection Window:** Open until notified last date
*   **Final Answer Key:** After scrutiny of objections (date to be notified)
*   **Result:** Within 2-3 weeks after the exam

**Exam Pattern:**

*   **Total Questions:** 180
*   **Total Marks:** 720
*   **Duration:** 180 minutes
*   **Sections:** English Language, Quantitative Aptitude, Data Sufficiency & Logical Reasoning, General Knowledge & Current Affairs

**Marking Scheme:** +4 marks for correct answers, potential negative marking (-1) for incorrect answers (confirm in official bulletin).

**How to Download:**

1.  Visit [cee.kerala.gov.in](cee.kerala.gov.in).
2.  Click on the “KMAT 2026” link under “Present Active Links”.
3.  Navigate to the “Answer Key” section.
4.  Download the PDF.

**Objection Process:** Candidates can challenge the provisional answer key with supporting documentation within the specified timeframe.  A fee may be applicable.

**Key Tip:** Always refer to the official answer key published by CEE, Kerala.

website_content.title: OPSC Dental Surgeon Recruitment 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## OPSC Dental Surgeon Recruitment 2026

The Odisha Public Service Commission (OPSC) has announced a recruitment drive for **124 Dental Surgeon** positions (including 41 for women) in Group-A (Junior Branch) of the Odisha Medical Services (Dental) Cadre.

**Key Details:**

*   **Organization:** Odisha Public Service Commission (OPSC)
*   **Department:** Health & Family Welfare Department, Government of Odisha
*   **Total Vacancies:** 124 (41 Women)
*   **Pay Level:** Level-12, Cell-1 of ORSP Rules, 2017 + DA
*   **Application Mode:** Online Only
*   **Advertisement No.:** 07 of 2025-26

**Important Dates:**

*   **Notice Release:** 09 March 2026
*   **Online Application Start:** 17 March 2026
*   **Online Application Last Date:** 17 April 2026 (05:00 PM)
*   **Written Examination:** 21 June 2026 (Sunday) - Tentative

**Eligibility:**

*   **Educational Qualification:** BDS or equivalent degree with valid registration under the Dentist Act, 1948.
*   **Age Limit:** 21-42 years (as of 01 January 2026). Age relaxation applies to reserved categories (up to 15 years for some).

**Selection Process:**

*   Written Test (MCQ based, 200 marks, 3 hours duration). Syllabus as per Dental Council of India (DCI).

**Fees:**

*   ₹700 for UR, SEBC, OBC, and other candidates.
*   NIL for SC, ST, and PwBD candidates.

**Apply Online:** [https://opsc.gov.in](https://opsc.gov.in)

**Don't miss this opportunity!**

website_content.markdown_content:
## Indian Navy Agniveer Recruitment 2026

The Indian Navy is seeking motivated individuals for Agniveer Posts (SSR, MR & SSR (Medical)).

**Key Details:**

*   **Posts:** Agniveer SSR, MR & SSR (Medical)
*   **Application Deadline:** 06-04-2026 (17:00 hrs)
*   **Apply Online:** [https://joinindiannavy.gov.in](https://joinindiannavy.gov.in)
*   **Start Date:** 14/03/2026 (00:01 hrs)
*   **Eligibility:** 10+2 with Physics, Chemistry, Biology (PCB) with 50% marks and minimum 40% in each subject.
*   **Age Limit:** Born between 01 May 2005 and 31 Oct 2009 (both dates inclusive).
*   **Salary:** Stipend of Rs. 14,600/- per month during training.

**Important Dates:**

*   **Application Window:** 14 March 2026 - 06 April 2026
*   **Correction Window:** 10 April 2026 - 11 April 2026
*   **INET Exam:** May 2026
*   **Result Declaration:** May – June 2026
*   **Stage II Shortlisting:** February 2027
*   **Stage II Exam:** March 2027
*   **Induction at INS Chilka:** May 2027

**Selection Process:**

1.  Stage I – INET (Indian Navy Entrance Test)
2.  Stage II (Physical Fitness Test, Written Examination and Recruitment Medical)

**Application Fee:** Rs. 550/- + 18% GST

Don't miss this opportunity to serve in the Indian Navy!

*   **Exam Name:** Bihar Combined Entrance Test (CET) B.Ed
*   **Duration:** 2 Hours
*   **Total Questions:** 120
*   **Total Marks:** 120
*   **Question Type:** OMR Based Objective Type

**Subject-wise Breakdown:**

*   General English Comprehension: 15 Questions (15 Marks)
*   General Sanskrit Comprehension (Shiksha Shastri): 15 Questions (15 Marks)
*   General Hindi: 15 Questions (15 Marks)
*   Logical & Analytical Reasoning: 25 Questions (25 Marks)
*   General Awareness: 40 Questions (40 Marks)
*   Teaching-Learning Environment in Schools: 25 Questions (25 Marks)

Download the detailed syllabus in PDF format from the official website. Prepare thoroughly for the exam!

website_content.title: Central Bank of India - Specialist Officer (SO) Recruitment 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## Central Bank of India Specialist Officer (SO) Recruitment 2026

The Central Bank of India is seeking experienced professionals for Specialist Officer (SO) positions in various domains including IT, Risk Management, Marketing, and Forex.

**Key Details:**

*   **Organization:** Central Bank of India
*   **Post:** Specialist Officer (SO)
*   **Exam Mode:** Online
*   **Exam Type:** Objective (MCQ)
*   **Total Questions:** 100
*   **Total Marks:** 100
*   **Duration:** 60 Minutes
*   **Negative Marking:** No
*   **Selection Process:** Online Exam + Interview

**Exam Pattern:**

| Section                                      | No. of Questions | Marks | Duration        |
| :------------------------------------------- | :--------------- | :---- | :-------------- |
| Stream/Category Specific (Professional Knowledge) | 70               | 70    | 60 Minutes      |
| Banking, Economy & General Awareness        | 30               | 30    | 60 Minutes      |
| **Total**                                    | **100**          | **100** | **60 Minutes** |

**Syllabus Highlights:**

*   **Professional Knowledge:** Stream-specific, 70 marks (IT, Risk Management, Marketing, Forex, etc.)
*   **Banking & Economy:** RBI functions, monetary policy, financial institutions, current affairs.
*   **Reasoning:** Logical reasoning, coding-decoding, etc.

**Selection Process:**

1.  Online Examination (Qualifying)
2.  Interview (Weightage often 70:30 for exam:interview)

**Important Note:** Focus 60-70% of your preparation on Professional Knowledge.

website_content.title: Karnataka SSLC Hall Ticket 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## Karnataka SSLC Hall Ticket 2026

**Overview:**

| Particulars | Details |
|---|---| 
| Exam Name | Karnataka SSLC Examination 2026 |
| Conducting Body | Karnataka School Examination and Assessment Board (KSEAB) |
| Hall Ticket Release Date | March 2026 |
| Mode of Availability | Online (School Login) |
| Official Website | kseeb.karnataka.gov.in |
| Exam Dates | March - April 2026 (Expected) |
| Login Credentials | School Code, User ID, and Password |

**How to Download:**

1. Go to the KSEAB SSLC portal: kseeb.karnataka.gov.in
2. Click on the “SSLC Hall Ticket 2026” link.
3. Enter the School Code, User ID, and Password.
4. Click “Login” and choose the option to generate hall tickets.
5. Download and print the admit card for all registered students.

**Important Note:** Students must collect their printed admit cards from school authorities after authentication.

**Details on Admit Card:**

*   Student’s Name and Roll Number
*   Photograph and Signature
*   School Name and Code
*   Exam Center Name and Code
*   Subject-wise Exam Dates and Timings
*   Important Instructions
*   Signature of the School Head and Board Seal

**Important Dates:**

| Event | Date (Expected) |
|---|---| 
| Hall Ticket Release Date | March 2026 |
| SSLC Exam Dates | March–April 2026 |
| Result Declaration | May 2026 (Tentative) |
| Supplementary Exam | June–July 2026 |

**Instructions:**

*   Verify name and subject spellings.
*   Keep multiple copies.
*   Read all exam instructions.
*   Locate your exam center in advance.
*   Do not write on the admit card.

*   **Posts:** Company Secretary, Programmer, Assistant Town Planner, Assistant Accounts Officer Grade-II, Junior Legal Officer, Personal Assistant Grade-II, Draughtsman-cum-Tracer (Civil)
*   **Selection Process:** Written Exam + Interview/Proficiency Test (post-dependent)

*   General Knowledge (Rajasthan GK, Indian History, Polity, Economy, Current Affairs)

*   Basic Computer Knowledge

**Preparation Tips:**

*   Download official syllabus PDFs.
*   Create a study timetable.
*   Practice previous year papers.
*   Take mock tests.
*   Focus on Rajasthan GK and Current Affairs.

website_content.title: Rajasthan Livestock Assistant Exam 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## Rajasthan Livestock Assistant Syllabus 2026

The Rajasthan Subordinate and Ministerial Services Selection Board (RSMSSB) has released the syllabus for the Livestock Assistant (Pashudhan Sahayak) Exam 2026. 

**Exam Overview:**

*   **Exam Name:** Rajasthan Livestock Assistant Exam 2026
*   **Conducting Body:** Rajasthan Subordinate and Ministerial Services Selection Board (RSMSSB)
*   **Post Name:** Livestock Assistant (Pashudhan Sahayak)
*   **Exam Mode:** Offline (OMR-based)
*   **Exam Type:** Objective (MCQ)
*   **Total Questions:** 150
*   **Total Marks:** 150
*   **Exam Duration:** 3 Hours
*   **Negative Marking:** Yes (1/3 mark deducted for each wrong answer)
*   **Selection Process:** Written Exam + Document Verification
*   **Exam Language:** English/Hindi

**Exam Pattern:**

| Subject/Section                | No. of Questions | Marks | Duration |
| :----------------------------- | :--------------- | :---- | :------- |
| General Knowledge & Current Affairs | 50               | 50    | 3 Hours  |
| Veterinary Science             | 100              | 100   |          |
| **Total**                      | **150**          | **150** |          |

**Syllabus Breakdown:**

*   **Part A: General Knowledge & Current Affairs** (History, Geography, Indian Political System, Economy of Rajasthan, Everyday Science, General Knowledge of Computers, Current Affairs)
*   **Part B: Veterinary Science** (Introductory Veterinary Anatomy, Physiology, Biochemistry, Animal Husbandry Extension, Medicine, Surgery, Nutrition, Management, Breeding, Reproduction, Pharmacology)

**Preparation Tips:**

*   Understand the syllabus and exam pattern.
*   Create a study timetable.
*   Refer to standard study materials.
*   Practice previous year question papers.
*   Take regular mock tests.
*   Stay updated with current affairs.

**Recommended Books:**

*   Lucent's General Knowledge
*   Rajasthan General Knowledge
*   Textbooks on Animal Husbandry & Veterinary Science
*   Pratiyogita Darpan / Monthly Magazines

website_content.title: IIT Bombay MDes Admission 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## IIT Bombay MDes Admission 2026 - Complete Guide

**Registration:** Opened March 9, 2026, closes March 23, 2026 (without late fee).

**Program:** Two-year MDes program offered by the Industrial Design Centre (IDC) at IIT Bombay.

**Streams:**
*   Animation Design
*   Communication Design
*   Industrial Design
*   Interaction Design
*   Mobility & Vehicle Design

**Eligibility:**
*   Bachelor's degree with 55% marks (50% for SC/ST/PwD)
*   Valid CEED 2026 score (exceptions for IIT graduates with CGPA 8.0+)
*   Accepted backgrounds: Design, Engineering, Architecture, Interior Design, BFA, MCA, MSc.

**Fee Structure:**
*   ₹300 (Gen male)
*   ₹150 (Reserved/Female) per stream

**Important Dates:**
*   Portal Opens: March 9, 2026
*   Regular Deadline: March 23, 2026
*   Late Fee Deadline: March 26, 2026
*   Shortlist for Test: April 20, 2026
*   Interviews: May 10–11, 2026
*   Final Merit List: June 1, 2026

**Selection Stages:**
*   CEED: 70% weightage
*   IDC Test & Interview: 30% weightage (includes portfolio review and studio exercises).

**Total Seats:** ~68 (varies by stream).

website_content.title: UGC NET December 2025 Final Answer Key Released
website_content.markdown_content:
## UGC NET December 2025 Final Answer Key Released!

The National Testing Agency (NTA) has released the UGC NET Final Answer Key 2026 for the December 2025 session. Candidates can now download the subject-wise final answer key PDFs.

**Key Highlights:**

| Detail | Information |
|---|---| 
| Exam Name | UGC NET December 2025 Session |
| Conducting Authority | National Testing Agency (NTA) |
| Exam Purpose | Eligibility for Assistant Professor & JRF in India |
| Exam Mode | Computer-Based Test (CBT) |
| Papers | Paper 1 (General) & Paper 2 (Subject-specific) |
| Final Answer Key Status | Released for UGC NET Dec 2025 session |
| Official Website | ugcnet.nta.ac.in |

**How to Download:**

1. Visit the official website: [ugcnet.nta.ac.in](ugcnet.nta.ac.in).
2. Navigate to the latest notification section.
3. Click on the link for “Final Answer Keys for UGC NET Dec 2025.”
4. Download the PDF for your subject (Paper 2) and Paper 1 (if provided separately).

**Important Dates:**

| Event | Details/Status |
|---|---| 
| UGC NET December 2025 Exam | Conducted in CBT mode |
| Provisional Answer Key Release | Released before challenge window |
| Answer Key Objection Window | Open for a limited period |
| Final Answer Key Release | Published after expert review |
| Result & Scorecard | Declared based on final answer key |

**Calculating Your Score:**

*   Download the final answer key and your response sheet (if available).
*   Compare your answers with the final key.
*   Count correct answers for Paper 1 and Paper 2.
*   Multiply correct answers by marks per question.
*   Add scores for both papers to estimate your total score.

**Remember:** Always refer to the official NTA website for the most accurate information.

website_content.title: RRB Group D Syllabus 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## RRB Group D Syllabus 2026

The Railway Recruitment Board (RRB) has released the detailed syllabus and exam pattern for Group D (Level-1) Exam 2026. Candidates preparing for the RRB Group D recruitment can download the official syllabus details, exam pattern, and marking scheme.

**Exam Overview:**

*   **Exam Name:** RRB Group D (Level-1) Exam 2026
*   **Conducting Body:** Railway Recruitment Board (RRB)
*   **Post Name:** Group D
*   **Total Vacancies:** 22195
*   **Exam Mode:** Online (Computer-Based Test - CBT)
*   **Exam Type:** Objective (MCQ)
*   **Total Questions:** 100
*   **Total Marks:** 100
*   **Exam Duration:** 90 Minutes (120 Minutes for PwBD candidates)
*   **Negative Marking:** Yes - 1/3 mark deducted for each wrong answer
*   **Selection Process:** CBT + Physical Efficiency Test (PET) + Document Verification + Medical Examination
*   **Exam Language:** English, Hindi, and 13 Regional Languages

**Exam Pattern (CBT Stage):**

| Subject / Section             | No. of Questions | Marks | Duration | 
| ----------------------------- | ---------------- | ----- | -------- | 
| Mathematics                   | 25               | 25    | 90 Minutes | 
| General Intelligence & Reasoning | 30               | 30    |          | 
| General Science               | 25               | 25    |          | 
| General Awareness & Current Affairs | 20               | 20    |          | 
| **Total**                     | **100**          | **100** |          | 

**Subject-Wise Topics:**

1.  **Mathematics:** Number System, Decimals & Fractions, LCM & HCF, Ratio & Proportion, Percentage, Mensuration, Time & Work, Time & Distance, Simple & Compound Interest, Profit & Loss, Algebra, Geometry & Trigonometry, Elementary Statistics, Square Root, Age Calculations, Calendar & Clock, Pipes & Cistern.
2.  **General Intelligence & Reasoning:** Analogies, Alphabetical & Number Series, Coding-Decoding, Mathematical Operations, Relationships (Blood Relations), Syllogism, Jumbling, Venn Diagram, Data Interpretation & Sufficiency, Conclusions & Decision Making, Similarities & Differences, Analytical Reasoning, Classification, Directions, Statement – Arguments & Assumptions, Puzzles.
3.  **General Science:** Physics, Chemistry, Biology (up to 10th CBSE/NCERT level).
4.  **General Awareness & Current Affairs:** Current Affairs (National & International - Last 6-12 months), Indian History, Geography, Polity & Constitution, Indian Economy & Budget, Static GK, Indian Railways & Railway Budget, Science & Technology Developments, Art & Culture of India, Important Government Schemes, Environmental Issues.

**Important Points:**

*   Each correct answer carries 1 mark.
*   Negative marking: 1/3 mark deducted for each wrong answer.
*   No marks for unattempted questions.
*   Minimum qualifying marks: UR/EWS - 40%, OBC/SC/ST - 30%, PwBD - 2% relaxation.

**Recommended Books:**

*   Mathematics: Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations - R.S. Aggarwal
*   Reasoning: A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning - R.S. Aggarwal
*   General Science: Lucent's General Science - Lucent Publications
*   General Awareness: Lucent's General Knowledge + Current Affairs Magazine - Lucent / Pratiyogita Darpan

website_content.markdown_content:
## PSSSB Clerk Syllabus 2026

The Punjab Subordinate Services Selection Board (PSSSB) has released the detailed syllabus for the Clerk Exam 2026. This guide covers the exam pattern, marking scheme, and subject-wise topics.

**Exam Overview:**

*   **Exam Name:** PSSSB Clerk Exam 2026
*   **Conducting Body:** Punjab Subordinate Services Selection Board (PSSSB)
*   **Post Name:** Clerk
*   **Exam Mode:** Online (Computer-Based Test)
*   **Exam Type:** Objective (MCQ)
*   **Total Questions:** 150
*   **Total Marks:** 150
*   **Exam Duration:** 150 Minutes
*   **Negative Marking:** Yes - 0.25 marks deducted for each wrong answer
*   **Selection Process:** Written Exam (CBT) + Document Verification + Typing Test (if applicable)
*   **Exam Language:** English / Punjabi

**Exam Pattern:**

| Subject/Section             | No. of Questions | Marks | 
| --------------------------- | ---------------- | ----- |
| General Knowledge / Awareness | 25               | 25    |
| Punjab History and Culture   | 17               | 17    |
| Logical Reasoning            | 25               | 25    |
| Punjabi Language             | 50               | 50    |
| Punjabi                      | 13               | 13    |
| English                      | 12               | 12    |
| ICT                          | 08               | 08    |
| **Total**                    | **150**          | **150** |

**Key Topics:**

*   **General Knowledge:** Current Affairs, Indian History, Punjab History, Economy, Science.
*   **Reasoning:** Analogies, Puzzles, Coding-Decoding.
*   **Mathematics:** Percentage, Ratio, Profit & Loss.
*   **Punjabi:** Grammar, Muhavare, Vakyansh.
*   **English:** Comprehension, Error Detection, Vocabulary.

**Preparation Tips:**

*   Understand the syllabus and exam pattern.
*   Create a study timetable.
*   Practice previous year papers.
*   Take mock tests.

| General English                  | 40         | 10             | 4               | 1             |

**Mathematics Topics:**

*   Set Theory
*   Probability and Statistics
*   Algebra
*   Coordinate Geometry
*   Calculus
*   Vectors
*   Trigonometry

**Computer Awareness Topics:**

*   Computer Basics
*   Data Representation
*   Binary Arithmetic

*   **Exam Type:** Objective (MCQ)
*   **Exam Duration:** 2 Hours
*   **Negative Marking:** Usually No (Confirm in official notification)
*   **Selection Process:** CBT + Computer Literacy/Skill Test (if applicable) + Document Verification

**Computer Literacy Test Focus (Accounts):**

*   MS-Word: Document creation, formatting, tables, mail merge.

*   General Computer Knowledge: Fundamentals, OS, internet, file management.

*   English Language

Download the official syllabus PDF and start preparing! Good luck!

## REET Mains Answer Key 2026 – Details

The Rajasthan Staff Selection Board (RSSB) has released the REET Mains Answer Key 2026 for the exam held from January 17th to 20th, 2026.

**Key Details:**

*   **Organization:** Rajasthan Staff Selection Board (RSSB)
*   **Post Name:** REET Mains (Primary & Upper Primary Teacher / 3rd Grade Teacher)
*   **Total Vacancies:** 7759 (5636 for Level-1, 2123 for Level-2)
*   **Exam Date:** 17th - 20th January 2026
*   **Answer Key Status:** Released (February 27, 2026)
*   **Objection Deadline:** Check official notification (typically a few days after release)
*   **Objection Fee:** ₹100 per question (refundable if accepted)
*   **Official Website:** [rssb.rajasthan.gov.in](rssb.rajasthan.gov.in)

**How to Download:**

1.  Visit [rssb.rajasthan.gov.in](rssb.rajasthan.gov.in).
2.  Navigate to the "Answer Key" or "Recruitment" section.
3.  Click on "REET Mains Answer Key 2026" (select Level 1 or Level 2).
4.  Log in with Registration Number and Password/Date of Birth.

**Scoring:**

*   Correct Answer: +2 marks
*   Wrong Answer: -1/3 mark
*   Unattempted: 0 marks

website_content.title: IRCTC Hospitality Monitors Recruitment 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## IRCTC Hospitality Monitors Recruitment 2026

**Company:** Indian Railway Catering & Tourism Corporation Ltd (IRCTC)

**Post Name:** Hospitality Monitors

**Number of Posts:** 43

**Salary:** Rs 30,000/- per month (including statutory deductions)

**Eligibility:**
* Full-time B.Sc. in Hospitality and Hotel Administration from a Central or State institute of Hotel Management (CIHM/ SIHM/PIHM) affiliated to NCHM&CT/ UGC/ AICTE/ Government of India.
* BBA/MBA (Culinary Arts) from Indian Culinary Institutes under Ministry of Tourism.
* B.Sc. Hotel Management and Catering Science from Government recognized Universities affiliated to UGC/AICTE/Government of India/State Government.
* M.B.A (Tourism and Hotel Management) from Government recognized Universities affiliated to UGC/AICTE/ Government of India/ State Government.

**Age Limit:**
* UR: 27 years
* SC/ST: 32 years
* OBC: 30 years
* PwBD: 37 years

**Walk-in Date:** 12-03-2026 to 13-03-2026

**Apply Mode:** Walk-in

**Job Type:** Contract

**Additional Benefits:**
* Daily Allowance: Rs 350/- per day (depending on duty hours)
* Lodging Charges: Rs.240/- (if night stay is involved)
* National Holiday Allowance: Rs 384/- per National Holiday
* Medical Insurance: Rs. 1,400/- to Rs 2,000/- per month (depending on age)

**Selection Process:**
1. Verification of Documents
2. Personal Interview
3. Verification of Antecedents
4. Medical Test

website_content.title: BSNL Senior Executive Trainee Recruitment 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## BSNL Senior Executive Trainee Syllabus 2026

The Bharat Sanchar Nigam Limited (BSNL) has released the syllabus for the Senior Executive Trainee Exam 2026 (Telecom and Finance Streams).

**Exam Overview:**

*   **Exam Name:** BSNL Senior Executive Trainee Exam 2026
*   **Conducting Body:** Bharat Sanchar Nigam Limited (BSNL)
*   **Post Name:** Senior Executive Trainee (Telecom Stream & Finance Stream)
*   **Total Vacancies:** 120
*   **Exam Mode:** Online (Computer-Based Test)
*   **Exam Type:** Objective (MCQ)
*   **Total Questions:** 200
*   **Total Marks:** 200
*   **Exam Duration:** 3 Hours (180 Minutes)
*   **Negative Marking:** Yes (0.25 marks deducted for each wrong answer)
*   **Selection Process:** Written Exam (CBT) + Document Verification + Medical Examination
*   **Exam Language:** English

**Exam Pattern:**

| Section                 | No. of Questions | Marks | Duration |
| ----------------------- | ---------------- | ----- | -------- |
| Aptitude Test (Part-I)  | 40               | 40    | 3 Hours  |
| Core Subject (Part-II) | 160              | 160   |          |
| **Total**               | **200**          | **200** |          |

**Syllabus Breakdown:**

*   **General Aptitude (40 Questions):** Decision Making, Reasoning, Arithmetical Ability, etc.
*   **Core Subject (160 Questions):** Varies by stream (Telecom, Finance).
    *   **Telecom Stream:** Network Analysis, Electronics, Digital Circuits, Measurements.
    *   **Finance Stream:** Financial Accounting, Taxation, Companies Act.

**Preparation Tips:**

*   Understand the syllabus and exam pattern.
*   Create a study timetable.
*   Practice previous year papers and mock tests.
*   Focus on accuracy due to negative marking.

**Recommended Books:**

*   Quantitative Aptitude: R.S. Aggarwal
*   Reasoning: R.S. Aggarwal
*   Telecom Engineering: Boylestad / Millman
*   Financial Management: I.M. Pandey

website_content.title: AIIMS Paramedical 2025 Syllabus & Exam Pattern Out
website_content.markdown_content:
## AIIMS Paramedical 2025: Important Updates

The All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS) has released the updated syllabus and exam pattern for the AIIMS Paramedical 2025 entrance examination.

**Key Details:**

*   **Exam Name:** Paramedical (Classes 11 & 12)
*   **Category:** Syllabus & Exam Pattern
*   **Status:** Released

**Exam Pattern:**

*   **Mode:** Computer-Based Test (CBT)
*   **Duration:** 90 minutes (10:00 AM - 11:30 AM)
*   **Total Questions:** 90
*   **Total Marks:** 90
*   **Question Type:** Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs)
*   **Marking Scheme:** +1 for correct, -1/3 for incorrect, 0 for unanswered.
*   **Subjects:** Physics (30), Chemistry (30), Biology/Mathematics (30)

**Syllabus Overview:**

*   **Physics:** Physical World & Measurement, Kinematics, Laws of Motion, Work, Energy & Power, etc.
*   **Chemistry:** Some Basic Concepts, Structure of Atom, Classification of Elements, Chemical Bonding, etc.
*   **Biology:** Diversity of Living Organisms, Structural Organisation, Cell Structure & Function, Plant Physiology, etc.

Candidates must attempt Physics and Chemistry. Choose either Biology or Mathematics based on course eligibility.

Download the complete syllabus and exam pattern from the official website.

website_content.title: RBSE 10th Result 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## RBSE 10th Result 2026

The Rajasthan Board of Secondary Education (BSER) will declare the RBSE 10th Result 2026. Students can check the official website for the direct result link and updates.

**Key Details:**

*   **Exam Dates:** February 12–28, 2026
*   **Result Date:** Expected late May/June 2026
*   **Official Site:** [https://rajeduboard.rajasthan.gov.in/](https://rajeduboard.rajasthan.gov.in/)
*   **Pass Percentage (2025):** Around 93%
*   **Students Appeared:** Approximately 10 lakh

**How to Download:**

1.  Visit [https://rajeduboard.rajasthan.gov.in/](https://rajeduboard.rajasthan.gov.in/)
2.  Navigate to the "Results" section.
3.  Select "Class 10th Main Exam Result 2026."
4.  Enter your roll number and date of birth.
5.  Download the PDF marksheet.

**What After Results?**

*   **Revaluation/Rechecking:** Apply within 7-15 days.
*   **Compartment Exams:** Held in July for failed subjects.
*   **Next Steps:** Choose Class 11 stream or explore skill-based courses.

website_content.title: RRB Paramedical Admit Card 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## RRB Paramedical Admit Card 2026 OUT!

The Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs) have released the admit card for the Paramedical Categories CBT 2026. The exam is scheduled to take place from **March 11th to 13th, 2026**. 

**Key Details:**

*   **Release Date:** March 8th, 2026
*   **Exam Dates:** March 11-13, 2026
*   **Total Vacancies:** 434 Posts
*   **Advertisement No.:** CEN 03/2025
*   **Exam Mode:** Online (Computer-Based Test)

**How to Download:**

1.  Visit the official RRB website of your region (e.g., rrbcdg.gov.in) or the centralized portal at rrb.digialm.com.
2.  Navigate to the "CEN 03/2025 (Paramedical)" or "Admit Card" section.
3.  Enter your Registration Number, Date of Birth, and Captcha Code.
4.  Download and print the admit card.  Keep multiple copies.

**Important Documents to Carry:**

*   Admit Card (printed copy)
*   Valid Photo ID Proof (Aadhaar, Voter ID, Driving License, PAN Card, Passport)
*   Recent passport-size photographs (2 copies)

**Important Instructions:**

*   Reach the exam center at least 60 minutes before reporting time.
*   Do not carry mobile phones, smartwatches, calculators, or any prohibited items.
*   Check all details on the admit card immediately after download and report any discrepancies to the RRB helpdesk within 24 hours.

**Helpdesk:** +91 7996104111 or rrbhelp@csc.gov.in

## GAIL Executive Trainee Recruitment 2026

**Gas Authority of India Limited (GAIL)** has announced a recruitment drive for **70 Executive Trainee** positions.

**Key Details:**

*   **Company:** Gas Authority of India (GAIL)
*   **Post Name:** Executive Trainee
*   **Number of Posts:** 70
*   **Advt No:** GAIL/OPEN/ET/2A/2025
*   **Salary:** Rs. 60,000 - 1,80,000/- per month
*   **Qualification:** Bachelor's Degree in Engineering (Chemical, Petrochemical, Instrumentation, Electrical, Mechanical, etc.) with minimum 65% marks (60% for SC/ST/PwBD).
*   **Age Limit:** 26 years (Relaxation applicable for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD).
*   **Last Date to Apply:** 18-03-2026
*   **Application Mode:** Online
*   **Job Type:** Full Time

**Vacancy Breakdown:**

*   Executive Trainee (Chemical): 17
*   Executive Trainee (Instrumentation): 21
*   Executive Trainee (Electrical): 16
*   Executive Trainee (Mechanical): 16

**Eligibility Criteria:**

*   Indian Nationals only.
*   Valid GATE-2026 score in relevant discipline (CH/IN/EE/ME) is mandatory.
*   Full-time regular courses from UGC/AICTE recognized institutions.

**Age Relaxation:**

*   SC/ST: 5 years
*   OBC(NCL): 3 years
*   PwBD (General/EWS): 10 years
*   PwBD (OBC): 13 years
*   PwBD (SC/ST): 15 years

**Selection Process:**

1.  Shortlisting based on GATE-2026 marks.
2.  Group Discussion/Personal Interview.
3.  Medical Fitness Examination.

The National Testing Agency (NTA) has released the final answer key for JEE Main Session 1 2026 for B.E./B.Tech candidates. The answer key is available for exams conducted between January 21st and 28th, 2026.

**Key Details:**

*   **Exam Name:** JEE Main Session 1 2026
*   **Exam Level:** B.E./B.Tech
*   **Release Date:** February 16, 2026
*   **Exam Dates Covered:** January 21, 22, 23, 24, and 28, 2026
*   **Shifts:** First and Second
*   **Centres:** India and Outside India

**How to Check:**

1.  Open the official answer key PDF.
2.  Locate your exam date and shift.
3.  Select the block corresponding to your exam centre (India or Outside India).
4.  Verify your answers using the Question ID and Correct Option ID.

The PDF is 20 pages long and is divided by date, shift, subject (Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry), and location.

## CUET PG Admit Card 2026 - Important Updates

The National Testing Agency (NTA) has released the exam city intimation slip for CUET PG 2026 exams scheduled from March 6-10, 2026. The admit card is expected to be released shortly.

**Key Details:**

*   **Exam Dates:** March 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 16, 17, 18, 19, 24, 25, and 27, 2026
*   **Mode:** Computer Based Test (CBT)
*   **Number of Shifts:** 44 shifts of 90 minutes each
*   **Website:** exams.nta.nic.in (CUET PG section) or nta.ac.in

**Login Credentials:**

*   Application Number
*   Password or Date of Birth
*   Security Pin

**Documents Required:**

*   Printed Admit Card (A4 size, preferably colour)
*   Original Photo ID (Aadhaar, PAN, Passport, Driving License, Voter ID)
*   Passport-size photograph (same as application form)
*   PwD certificate (if applicable)

**Important Reminders:**

*   Reach the exam center 60-90 minutes before reporting time.
*   Do not carry electronic gadgets, study material, or metallic items.
*   Check all details on the admit card carefully and contact NTA helpdesk for any discrepancies.

website_content.markdown_content:
## PSSSB Clerk IT & Hostel Superintendent Answer Key 2026 Released!

The Punjab Subordinate Services Selection Board (PSSSB) has released the provisional answer key for the Clerk IT and Hostel Superintendent cum PTI examination held on **February 28, 2026**. 

**Key Details:**
*   **Organization:** Punjab Subordinate Services Selection Board (PSSSB)
*   **Posts:** Clerk IT (03 vacancies), Hostel Superintendent cum PTI (68 vacancies)
*   **Total Vacancies:** 71 (Advt No. 11/2025 & 13/2025)
*   **Answer Key Released On:** March 01, 2026
*   **Objection Deadline:** Check official notice on sssb.punjab.gov.in

**How to Download:**
1.  Visit [sssb.punjab.gov.in](sssb.punjab.gov.in).
2.  Navigate to the "Results" or "Latest News/Updates" section.
3.  Click on the relevant "Provisional Answer Key" link.
4.  Login with Roll Number/Registration Number and Date of Birth to view your OMR/response sheet.

**How to Raise Objections:**
1.  Login to your candidate portal on sssb.punjab.gov.in.
2.  Go to the "Objection" / "Challenge" section.
3.  Select the question(s) and upload supporting documents (if required).
4.  Pay the objection fee (if applicable).

**Important Dates:**
*   Exam Date: February 28, 2026
*   Answer Key Release Date: March 01, 2026
*   Objection Deadline: As per official notice

**Marking Scheme:**
*   Correct Answer: +1 mark
*   Wrong Answer: -0.25 mark (negative marking)
*   Unattempted: 0 marks

website_content.markdown_content:
## IIT Delhi Admissions 2026

Applications are open for PhD and PG programs (1st Semester 2026-27) at IIT Delhi!

**Programs:** PhD, MTech, MS(R), MDes
**Key Exams:** GATE 2026, CEED, IIT JAM, NET/JRF (PhD flexible)
**Portal:** [https://ecampus.iitd.ac.in/IPGADM/login](https://ecampus.iitd.ac.in/IPGADM/login)
**Mode:** Online application, shortlist, test/interview

*   Portal Opens: March 2026 (ongoing)
*   Last Date (Foreign): March 30, 2026 (4 PM)
*   Shortlist: April/May 2026
*   Test/Interview: May 2026
*   Results: May/June 2026

**Eligibility (Highlights):**
*   PhD: Master's (60% or BTech 75%) + GATE/NET
*   MTech/MS(R): BTech/BSc (60%) + GATE
*   MDes: Bachelor's (55-60%) + CEED

**Application Fee:** ₹200-1000

**Selection Process:** Shortlisting based on academics/exam scores, followed by RAT/interview. PhD emphasizes research fit.

**Resources:**

*   [PG Admissions](https://ecampus.iitd.ac.in/IPGADM/login)

website_content.markdown_content:
## NTA NIFTEE 2026 Provisional Answer Key – Important Details

The National Testing Agency (NTA) has released the Provisional Answer Key for the NIFT Entrance Examination (NIFTEE) 2026 – General Ability Test (GAT) paper on **February 17, 2026**. Candidates can access their response sheets and the answer key on the official portal.

**Key Dates:**

*   **Exam Date:** February 8, 2026
*   **Answer Key & Response Sheet Release:** February 17, 2026
*   **Objection Window:** February 17 – February 19, 2026 (11:50 PM)
*   **Final Answer Key:** Expected March – April 2026

**How to Download:**

1.  Visit [exams.nta.nic.in/niftee](exams.nta.nic.in/niftee).
2.  Click on the link for “Display of Provisional Answer Keys and Recorded Response Sheet…”.
3.  Login using Application Number/Roll Number, Date of Birth, and security pin.
4.  Download the answer key PDF and response sheet.

**Objection Process:**

*   **Fee:** ₹200 per question
*   **Deadline:** February 19, 2026 (11:50 PM)

**Programmes:** B.Des, B.F.Tech, M.Des, M.F.Tech, and MFM.

**Note:** No answer key is released for the CAT (Creative Ability Test).

## TN SSLC Hall Ticket 2026 – Download Now!

The Directorate of Government Examinations (DGE), Tamil Nadu has released the TN SSLC Hall Ticket 2026 for students appearing in the Class 10 public exams. 

**Key Details:**

*   **Exam Name:** Tamil Nadu SSLC (10th Class) Exam 2026
*   **Conducting Body:** Directorate of Government Examinations (DGE), Tamil Nadu
*   **Release Date:** March 2026
*   **Exam Dates:** March - April 2026
*   **Mode of Release:** Online
*   **Official Website:** tn.gov.in

**How to Download:**

1.  Visit the official website.
2.  Click on “SSLC Hall Ticket 2026”.
3.  Select your school type and enter your registration number and date of birth.
4.  Click “Submit”.
5.  Download and print the hall ticket.

**Information on Hall Ticket:**

*   Student’s name and roll number
*   Date of birth
*   School name and code
*   Subject names and codes
*   Exam center name and address
*   Exam dates and timings

**Exam Schedule (Tentative):**

*   Language: 16th March 2026
*   English: 20th March 2026
*   Mathematics: 23rd March 2026
*   Science (Physical Science): 25th March 2026
*   Science (Biological Science): 28th March 2026
*   Social Science: 30th March 2026
*   Vocational Course: 1st April 2026

**Important Instructions:**

*   Carry a printed copy of your hall ticket.
*   Report to the exam hall 30 minutes before the start time.
*   Avoid mobile phones, smart watches, or calculators.

website_content.title: EMRS Staff Nurse Exam 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## EMRS Staff Nurse Syllabus 2026

The Eklavya Model Residential School (EMRS) under the National Education Society for Tribal Students (NESTS) has released the syllabus for the Staff Nurse Exam 2026.

**Exam Overview:**

*   **Exam Name:** EMRS Staff Nurse Exam 2026
*   **Conducting Body:** National Education Society for Tribal Students (NESTS) / EMRS
*   **Post Name:** Staff Nurse
*   **Exam Mode:** Offline (Computer-Based Test)
*   **Exam Type:** Objective (MCQ)
*   **Total Questions:** 100
*   **Total Marks:** 100
*   **Exam Duration:** Prelims: 2 Hours, Mains: 3 Hours
*   **Negative Marking:** No
*   **Selection Process:** Written Exam (CBT) + Document Verification
*   **Exam Language:** English / Hindi

**Exam Pattern:**

| Part | Subject | No. of Questions | Marks | Duration |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| I | General Awareness / General Knowledge | 10 | 10 | 2 Hours |
| II | Reasoning Ability | 15 | 15 | |
| III | Knowledge of ICT | 15 | 15 | |
| IV | Quantitative Aptitude | 15 | 15 | |
| V | Language Competency | 30 | 30 | |
| **Total** |  | **100** | **100** | |

**Key Topics:**

*   **General Awareness:** Current Affairs, Indian History, Geography, Polity, Economy, General Science, Indian Culture, Environmental Issues, Sports.
*   **Reasoning:** Analogies, Spatial Orientation, Problem Solving, Judgment, Coding-Decoding.
*   **Knowledge of ICT:** Basics of Computer, MS Office, Internet & Email, Computer Networks.
*   **Quantitative Aptitude:** Basic mathematical concepts.
*   **Language Competency:** English and/or Hindi.

**Preparation Tips:**

*   Focus 60-70% time on Nursing Subjects.
*   Create a realistic study timetable.
*   Practice previous year question papers and mock tests.
*   Stay updated with current affairs.

**Important Note:** Always refer to the official website [emrs.tribal.gov.in](emrs.tribal.gov.in) for the latest updates and complete syllabus.

website_content.title: Rajasthan Police SI Recruitment 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## Rajasthan Police SI Syllabus 2026

The Rajasthan Police SI Recruitment 2026 is a state-level exam conducted by the Rajasthan Public Service Commission (RPSC) for Sub-Inspector posts in Rajasthan Police.

**Key Details:**

*   **Exam Name:** Rajasthan Police Sub-Inspector (SI) Recruitment 2026
*   **Conducting Authority:** Rajasthan Public Service Commission (RPSC)
*   **Exam Level:** State-level competitive examination
*   **Mode of Exam:** Offline/OMR-based Written Exam (Objective type)
*   **Selection Stages:** Written Exam, Physical Measurement Test (PMT), Physical Efficiency Test (PET), Interview, Document Verification, Medical Examination
*   **Total Written Marks:** 400 marks (200 + 200)

**Exam Pattern:**

| Paper | Subject                      | Questions | Marks | Duration | Type        |
|-------|------------------------------|-----------|-------|----------|-------------|
| I     | General Hindi                | 100       | 200   | 2 hours  | Objective (MCQ) |
| II    | General Knowledge & Science | 100       | 200   | 2 hours  | Objective (MCQ) |

*   **Marking Scheme:** +2 marks for correct answer, -1/3 for incorrect answer.
*   **Negative Marking:** 1/3 marks deducted for each wrong answer.

**Physical Standards:**

*   **Male:** Height: 168 cm, Chest: 81 cm (unexpanded) to 86 cm (expanded)
*   **Female:** Height: 152 cm

**Resources:**

*   Standard Hindi grammar books
*   Rajasthan GK books
*   NCERT textbooks (Class 6–10) for science and social science
*   Previous year papers

website_content.title: RPF SI Exam 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## RPF SI Syllabus 2026

The Railway Protection Force (RPF) has released the detailed syllabus and exam pattern for the Sub-Inspector (SI) Exam 2026. Candidates preparing for the RPF SI recruitment can download the official syllabus details, exam pattern, and marking scheme.

**Exam Overview:**

*   **Exam Name:** RPF Sub-Inspector (SI) Exam 2026
*   **Conducting Body:** Railway Recruitment Board (RRB) / Railway Protection Force (RPF)
*   **Post Name:** Sub-Inspector
*   **Exam Mode:** Online (Computer-Based Test - CBT)
*   **Exam Type:** Objective (MCQ)
*   **Total Questions:** 120
*   **Total Marks:** 120
*   **Exam Duration:** 90 Minutes
*   **Negative Marking:** Yes - 1/3 mark deducted for each wrong answer
*   **Selection Process:** CBT + Physical Efficiency Test (PET) / Physical Measurement Test (PMT) + Document Verification + Medical Examination
*   **Exam Language:** English, Hindi, and 13 Regional Languages

**Exam Pattern (CBT Stage):**

| Subject / Section | No. of Questions | Marks | Duration |
|---|---|---|---|
| General Awareness | 50 | 50 | 90 Minutes |
| Arithmetic | 35 | 35 | |
| General Intelligence & Reasoning | 35 | 35 | |
| **Total** | **120** | **120** | |

**Syllabus Breakdown:**

*   **General Awareness:** Indian History, Art & Culture, Indian Geography & Polity, Indian Economy & Current Affairs, General Science, Static GK, Indian Railways, Government Schemes, Environmental Issues, Indian Constitution, Current Events related to Railways & Security.
*   **Arithmetic:** Number System, Decimals & Fractions, LCM & HCF, Ratio & Proportion, Percentage, Mensuration, Time & Work, Time & Distance, Simple & Compound Interest, Profit & Loss, Algebra, Geometry, Statistics.
*   **General Intelligence & Reasoning:** Analogies, Series, Coding-Decoding, Mathematical Operations, Blood Relations, Syllogism, Venn Diagram, Data Interpretation, Conclusions, Similarities & Differences, Analytical Reasoning, Classification, Directions & Ranking, Statement – Arguments & Assumptions, Puzzles, Non-Verbal Reasoning.

**Important Points:**

*   Minimum qualifying marks: UR/EWS - 35%, OBC/SC/ST - 30% (may vary).
*   Physical Test: Running (1600m for males).

**Recommended Books:**

*   Arithmetic: Quantitative Aptitude by R.S. Aggarwal
*   Reasoning: A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning by R.S. Aggarwal
*   General Awareness: Lucent's General Knowledge
*   General Science: Lucent's General Science

website_content.markdown_content:
## Indian Army Agniveer Recruitment 2026

The Indian Army is recruiting for Agniveer posts (GD, Tradesman, Clerk & Other Posts). Interested and eligible candidates can apply online.

**Key Details:**

*   **Company:** Indian Army
*   **Post Name:** Agniveer (GD, Tradesman, Clerk & Other Posts)
*   **Number of Posts:** 25000+ (Expected)
*   **Salary:**
    *   1st Year: Rs. 30,000/- 
    *   2nd Year: Rs. 33,000/- 
    *   3rd Year: Rs. 36,500/- 
    *   4th Year: Rs. 40,000/- 
*   **Qualification:** 12TH, 10TH, 8TH
*   **Age Limit:** 17.5 to 22 years (Born between 01 Jul 2005 to 01 Jul 2009)
*   **Last Date to Apply:** 01-04-2026
*   **Application Fee:** Rs 250/- 
*   **Selection Process:** Online Common Entrance Exam (CEE) & Recruitment Rally (PFT, Medical, Document Verification)

**Important Dates:**

*   **Application Start Date:** 13-02-2026
*   **Application End Date:** 01-04-2026
*   **Online Exam Dates:** 01/06/2026 to 15/06/2026 (Tentative)

**Documents Required:** Admit Card, Education Certificates, Domicile Certificate, Caste Certificate (if applicable), and other documents as specified in the notification.

**Physical Standards:**
* Height: 162 cms
* Chest Expansion: 05 cms

website_content.title: CPCT 2026 Admit Card
website_content.markdown_content:
## CPCT 2026 Admit Card - Expected Soon!

The Computer Proficiency Certification Test (CPCT) admit card for the 2026 exam is expected to be released soon on the official website.

**Key Details:**

*   **Exam Dates:** 13-03-2026 to 15-03-2026
*   **Admit Card Status:** Expected Soon
*   **Official Website:** [Click Here](https://cpct.mp.gov.in)

**How to Download:**

1.  Visit the official website: cpct.mp.gov.in
2.  Click on the CPCT Admit Card 2026 link.
3.  Enter your Application Number and Date of Birth.
4.  Download and print your admit card.

**Important Instructions:**

*   Verify all details on the admit card.
*   Carry a valid photo ID (Aadhaar, Voter ID, or Passport).
*   Reach the exam center before the reporting time.
*   Do not carry prohibited items like mobile phones.

**Details Mentioned on Admit Card:**

*   Candidate's name and roll number
*   Registration number and course details
*   Exam date and reporting time
*   Exam center name and address
*   Photograph and signature
*   Subject/paper details
*   Important instructions

website_content.title: RBI Assistant Recruitment 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## RBI Assistant Recruitment 2026

The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has announced a recruitment drive for **650 Assistant posts** across various offices in India.

**Important Dates:**
*   Application Start Date: February 16, 2026
*   Application Last Date: March 08, 2026
*   Preliminary Examination: April 11, 2026 (Tentative)
*   Main Examination: June 07, 2026 (Tentative)

**Exam Pattern:**

**Prelims (100 marks, 60 mins):**
*   English Language (30 questions, 30 marks, 20 mins)
*   Numerical Ability (35 questions, 35 marks, 20 mins)
*   Reasoning Ability (35 questions, 35 marks, 20 mins)

**Mains (200 marks, 135 mins):**
*   Test of Reasoning (40 questions, 40 marks, 30 mins)
*   Test of English Language (40 questions, 40 marks, 30 mins)
*   Test of Numerical Ability (40 questions, 40 marks, 30 mins)
*   Test of General Awareness (40 questions, 40 marks, 25 mins)
*   Test of Computer Knowledge (40 questions, 40 marks, 20 mins)

**Syllabus:** Detailed syllabus available for Prelims and Mains covering topics like English, Numerical Ability, Reasoning, General Awareness, and Computer Knowledge.

**LPT:** Language Proficiency Test is mandatory and qualifying for shortlisted candidates.

**Negative Marking:** ¼ (0.25) for each wrong answer in both Prelims and Mains.

*   **Scheduled Caste (SC):** 30

website_content.title: PU MET 2025 Syllabus Out
website_content.markdown_content:
## PU MET 2025 Syllabus & Exam Details

The syllabus for the Panjab University Management Entrance Test (PU MET) 2025 is now available!

**Exam Date:** April 27, 2025 (Offline Mode)

**Course:** MBA

**Exam Pattern:**

| Subject | Total Questions | Total Marks | Duration |
|---|---|---|---| 
| General Knowledge | 200 | 200 | 2 hours |
| Economics and Business Environment Awareness | - | - | - |
| Data Interpretation and Problem Solving | - | - | - |
| Numerical Ability | - | - | - |
| Verbal Ability / Reasoning | - | - | - |
| English Comprehension | - | - | - |
| **Overall** | 200 | 200 | 2 hours |

**Key Topics:**

*   **General Knowledge:** Current Affairs, Static GK, History, Geography, Science, Economy, Politics.
*   **English Language:** Grammar, Reading Comprehension, Vocabulary, Idioms & Phrases.
*   **Logical Reasoning:** Blood Relations, Puzzles, Coding-Decoding, Direction Sense.
*   **Quantitative Ability:** Percentages, Numbers, Geometry, Ratio, Time-Speed-Distance, Algebra.
*   **Data Interpretation:** Bar Graphs, Line Graphs, Data Sufficiency, Pie Charts.

**Preparation Tips:**

*   Understand the exam pattern.
*   Break down the syllabus section-wise.
*   Create a study schedule.
*   Practice mock tests.
*   Focus on weak areas.
*   Stay updated with current affairs.

website_content.title: HPRCA JBT Answer Key 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## HPRCA JBT Answer Key 2026 Released!

The Himachal Pradesh Rajya Chayan Aayog (HPRCA) has released the answer key for the Junior Basic Teacher (JBT) examination held from February 25th to 27th, 2026. 

**Key Details:**

*   **Organization:** Himachal Pradesh Rajya Chayan Aayog (HPRCA)
*   **Post Name:** JBT (Junior Basic Teacher)
*   **Total Vacancies:** 600
*   **Exam Date:** February 25th - 27th, 2026
*   **Answer Key Released:** March 2nd, 2026
*   **Objection Deadline:** March 8th, 2026 (11:55 PM)

**How to Download:**

1.  Visit [hprca.hp.gov.in](hprca.hp.gov.in)
2.  Navigate to the "Recruitment for the post of Junior Basic Teacher" or "Answer Key/Notices" section.
3.  Click on "HPRCA JBT Answer Key 2026" or the login link.
4.  Login with your Application Number and User Password.

**How to Raise Objections:**

1.  Log in to your candidate portal on hprca.hp.gov.in.
2.  Go to the "Objection" or "Challenge/Objection" section.
3.  Select the question(s) to challenge and upload supporting documents.
4.  Pay the objection fee (if applicable).
5.  Submit before March 8th, 2026 (11:55 PM).

**Important Dates:**

*   Exam Conducted: February 25th - 27th, 2026
*   Answer Key Released: March 2nd, 2026
*   Objection Deadline: March 8th, 2026
*   Final Answer Key (Expected): March/April 2026
*   Result Declaration (Expected): April 2026 onwards

## JKPSC Assistant Professor Syllabus 2026

The Jammu and Kashmir Public Service Commission (JKPSC) has released the syllabus for Assistant Professor positions in Neurosurgery, Medical Oncology, and Urology at SKIMS Soura Srinagar.

**Exam Details:**

*   **Exam Name:** JKPSC Assistant Professor Exam 2026 (Health & Medical Education Department)
*   **Conducting Body:** Jammu and Kashmir Public Service Commission (JKPSC)
*   **Post Name:** Assistant Professor (Neurosurgery, Medical Oncology, Urology)
*   **Exam Mode:** Offline / Online (Written Examination)
*   **Exam Type:** Objective (MCQ) / Descriptive
*   **Total Questions/Marks:** Varies by Post (120 Questions / 120-225 Marks)
*   **Exam Duration:** 2-3 Hours
*   **Negative Marking:** Yes/No (Refer to official notification)
*   **Selection Process:** Written Exam + Interview/Viva-Voce + Document Verification
*   **Exam Language:** English

**Syllabus PDFs:**

*   [Assistant Professor (Neurosurgery) Syllabus](Download Here)
*   [Assistant Professor (Medical Oncology) Syllabus](Download Here)
*   [Assistant Professor (Urology) Syllabus](Download Here)

**Key Preparation Tips:**

*   Refer to standard postgraduate textbooks.
*   Stay updated with recent advances in journals.
*   Practice MCQs from previous exams.
*   Prepare for the interview (teaching experience, research).

**Recommended Books:**

*   **Neurosurgery:** Youmans and Winn Neurological Surgery
*   **Medical Oncology:** DeVita, Hellman, and Rosenberg's Cancer
*   **Urology:** Campbell-Walsh-Wein Urology

*   Wrong Answer: 0 marks
*   Unattempted: 0 marks

website_content.title: CBSE Class 11 Chemistry Syllabus 2025 Released
website_content.markdown_content:
## CBSE Class 11 Chemistry Syllabus 2025

The CBSE Class 11 Chemistry Syllabus 2025 has been officially released! This syllabus provides a comprehensive understanding of fundamental chemistry principles and their real-world applications.

**Key Highlights:**

*   **Sections:** Theory & Practical
*   **Theory Topics:**
    *   Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry
    *   Structure of Atom
    *   Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
    *   Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure
    *   States of Matter: Gases and Liquids
    *   Thermodynamics
    *   Equilibrium
    *   Redox Reactions
    *   Hydrocarbons
    *   Environmental Chemistry
*   **Practical Evaluation:**
    *   Volumetric Analysis (08 marks)
    *   Salt Analysis (08 marks)
    *   Content Based Experiment (06 marks)
    *   Project Work (04 marks)
    *   Class record and viva (04 marks)

**Class XII (2024-25) Theory Topics:**
*   Solutions
*   Electrochemistry
*   Chemical Kinetics
*   d- and f-Block Elements
*   Coordination Compounds
*   Haloalkanes and Haloarenes
*   Alcohols, Phenols and Ethers
*   Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids
*   Amines
*   Biomolecules

**How to Download:**
1.  Visit the official CBSE website (cbse.gov.in).
2.  Navigate to the "Curriculum" section under "Academics".
3.  Select "Senior Secondary Curriculum".
4.  Choose the "Class 11" syllabus for the academic year.
5.  Download the Chemistry syllabus PDF.

website_content.title: JPSC Drug Inspector Exam 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## JPSC Drug Inspector Syllabus 2026

The Jharkhand Public Service Commission (JPSC) has released the detailed syllabus for the Drug Inspector Exam 2026. This guide covers the exam pattern, marking scheme, and subject-wise topics.

**Exam Overview:**

*   **Exam Name:** JPSC Drug Inspector Exam 2026
*   **Conducting Body:** Jharkhand Public Service Commission (JPSC)
*   **Post Name:** Drug Inspector
*   **Total Vacancies:** 30
*   **Exam Mode:** Offline (Written Examination - Objective)
*   **Exam Type:** Objective (MCQ)
*   **Total Marks:** 200 Marks per paper, 1000 total
*   **Exam Duration:** 2 Hours per Paper
*   **Selection Process:** Written Exam + Interview / Viva-Voce + Document Verification
*   **Exam Language:** English / Hindi

**Exam Pattern:**

| Paper / Section | Subject                          | No. of Marks | Duration |
|----------------|----------------------------------|--------------|----------|
| Paper I         | Pharmaceutics / General Medicine | 200          | 2 Hours  |
| Paper II        | Microbiology                      | 200          | 2 Hours  |
| Paper III       | Pharmacology                      | 200          | 2 Hours  |
| Paper IV        | Pharmaceutical Chemistry / Biochemistry | 200          | 2 Hours  |
| Paper V         | Pharmaceutical Jurisprudence & Ethics | 200          | 2 Hours  |
| **Total**       |                                  | **1000**     | **10 Hours** |

**Key Topics:**

*   Drugs & Cosmetics Act 1940 & Rules 1945
*   Pharmaceutical Jurisprudence & Ethics
*   Pharmacology & Drug Actions
*   Medicinal Chemistry & SAR
*   Jharkhand-specific Current Affairs & GK
*   Pharmaceutics (Dosage Forms & GMP)

**Preparation Tips:**

*   Focus on technical Pharmacy subjects.
*   Study Drugs & Cosmetics Act thoroughly.
*   Practice MCQs regularly.
*   Stay updated with Jharkhand GK.

**Recommended Books:**

*   Pharmaceutical Jurisprudence: Forensic Pharmacy / Pharmaceutical Jurisprudence by B.S. Kuchekar / Jain & Gupta
*   Pharmacology: Essentials of Medical Pharmacology by K.D. Tripathi
*   Pharmaceutics: Physical Pharmaceutics / Remington: The Science and Practice of Pharmacy by C.V.S. Subrahmanyam / Remington

website_content.markdown_content:
## AIIMS BSc Nursing Syllabus 2025 Released!

The All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS) has released the updated syllabus for the AIIMS BSc Nursing Entrance Exam 2025. This syllabus is essential for candidates preparing for BSc (H) Nursing, BSc Nursing (Post-Basic), and BSc Paramedical courses.

**Key Details:**

*   **Subjects Covered:** Physics, Chemistry, Biology, and General Knowledge.
*   **Exam Pattern:**
    *   Physics: 30 Questions
    *   Chemistry: 30 Questions
    *   Biology: 30 Questions
    *   General Knowledge: 10 Questions
    *   Total: 100 Questions
    *   Duration: 2 hours (120 minutes)
*   **Key Topics:**
    *   **Physics:** Current Electricity, Electromagnetic Induction, Electronic Devices, and more.
    *   **Chemistry:** Solid State, Biomolecules, Polymers, and more.
    *   **Biology:** Cell Theory, Mendel’s Laws of Inheritance, Five Kingdom Classification, and more.
    *   **General Knowledge:** Current Affairs, History, Geography, Culture.

Download the syllabus and start your preparation today!

website_content.title: CSEB Kerala Recruitment 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## CSEB Kerala Recruitment 2026 - 94 Vacancies

Cooperative Service Examination Board, Kerala (CSEB Kerala) is recruiting for 94 positions including Data Entry Operator, Secretary, and more.

**Important Details:**

*   **Organization:** Cooperative Service Examination Board, Kerala (CSEB Kerala)
*   **Posts:** Junior Clerk / Cashier, Assistant Secretary / Chief Accountant, Secretary, System Administrator, Data Entry Operator
*   **Total Vacancies:** 94
*   **Advt Nos:** 8/2026, 9/2026, 10/2026, 11/2026, 12/2026, 13/2026, 14/2026
*   **Salary:** ₹7,900 – ₹69,250 per month (varies by post)
*   **Eligibility:** Any Graduate, B.Sc, M.Com, MBA/PGDM, MCA/B.Tech
*   **Age Limit:** 18-40 years (as of 01/01/2026) - Age relaxation applicable.
*   **Application Start:** 05/03/2026
*   **Application End:** 04/04/2026
*   **Application Mode:** Online

**Vacancy Breakdown:**

*   Data Entry Operator (Advt 14/2026): 3 Posts
*   System Administrator (Advt 13/2026): 1 Post
*   Junior Clerk/Cashier (Class-2 to Class-7 Banks) (Advt 12/2026): 21 Posts
*   Junior Clerk/Cashier – Special Grade, Class-I Banks (Advt 11/2026): 39 Posts
*   Junior Clerk/Cashier – Urban Grade Banks (Advt 10/2026): 21 Posts
*   Assistant Secretary / Chief Accountant (Advt 9/2026): 6 Posts
*   Secretary (Advt 8/2026): 3 Posts

**Selection Process:** Written Exam (100 marks) + Interview (20 marks). 

**Application Fee:** ₹150 (General) / ₹50 (SC/ST) + 18% GST. Additional bank/society applications cost ₹50 each.

**Apply Online:** Visit cseb.kerala.gov.in
